data.title: Pacific West Bancorp reported a net income of $126K for Q4 2025, $646K for 2025.
data.description: In Portland, Oregon on January 28, 2026, Pacific West Bancorp announced that the holding company of Pacific West Bank reported a net income of $126,000 or $0.05 per diluted share for the quarter ending on December 31, 2025. The net income for the entire year of 2025 was $646,000.

data.title: BMO to implement reverse splits of 2 ETNs (GDXD and FNGD) listed on NYSE Arca. Details to follow.
data.description: Today, Bank of Montreal (BMO) disclosed its plans to carry out reverse splits for two series of its Exchange Traded Notes (ETNs). This move will affect multiple ETNs and aims to streamline their performance. The reverse splits will involve notable adjustments to the series listed in the forthcoming table. This strategic decision by BMO is part of its ongoing efforts to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of its ETNs. Investors and stakeholders can anticipate significant changes in the structure and operation of these ETNs following the implementation of these reverse splits, which are projected to have a considerable impact on their overall performance.

data.title: Consumer Watchdog investigates insurance companies mistreating wildfire survivors post Los Angeles fires.
data.description: A new report has been released by Consumer Watchdog in Los Angeles on Jan. 28, 2026, marking the one-year anniversary of the devastating Los Angeles fires. This final report is part of a three-part series that delves into the practices of major insurance companies in underpaying wildfire survivors. The investigation highlights how these companies use subcontractors aligned with the industry and obscure methods to shortchange the victims. This reveals the alarming trend of insurers exploiting the situation, leaving survivors with inadequate compensation.

data.title: Comstock Inc. plans public stock offering, granting underwriter option to purchase additional shares; proceeds to fund new facility, refining process, and market growth.
data.description: Comstock Inc. (NYSE: LODE) recently announced the initiation of an underwritten public offering to sell its common stock. This offering includes a 30-day option for the underwriter to purchase up to 15% additional common stock to cover over-allotments. The net proceeds from the sale will primarily go towards funding capital expenditure requirements for Comstock Metals LLC's second large-scale facility, refining process development, site selections, and market growth initiatives. Any remaining funds will be allocated for general corporate purposes. The completion, size, and terms of the offering are dependent on market conditions and other variables, with no guarantee of its finalization.

data.title: Franco-Nevada to announce year-end 2025 results on March 10th after market close, with conference call at March 11th 10:00 am ET.
data.description: Today, Franco-Nevada Corporation revealed its plan to disclose its year-end 2025 results on March 10th post market close. A conference call is scheduled for March 11th at 10:00 am Eastern Time for discussing the financial outcomes. Participants can join the call by dialing 1-888-510-2154 for toll-free access.
Expion360 releases estimated 2025 financial results, awaiting audit.

data.title: Riverview Bancorp, Inc. launches $4.0 million stock repurchase plan on Jan 28, 2026. HQ in Vancouver, WA.
data.description: In Vancouver, Washington, Riverview Bancorp, Inc. (Nasdaq GSM: RVSB), the parent company of Riverview Bank, revealed that on January 22, 2026, its Board of Directors approved a stock buyback plan. The company, based in Vancouver, WA, is taking this step to repurchase shares of its own stock.

data.title: Shore Bancshares, Inc. announced Q4 2025 net income of $15.9 million, $0.48 per share. Hold company for Shore United Bank.
data.description: In Easton, Maryland, on January 28, 2026, Shore Bancshares, Inc. (NASDAQ: SHBI), the parent company of Shore United Bank, N.A., disclosed fourth-quarter 2025 earnings. The company reported a net income of $15.9 million, equivalent to $0.48 per diluted common share.

data.title: DeFi Development Corp. (Nasdaq: DFDV) to host X Spaces event on Jan. 29, 2025 at 11:30 AM ET discussing progress and future plans.
data.description: Boca Raton, Florida - January 28, 2026 - DeFi Development Corp. (Nasdaq: DFDV), the pioneering U.S. public firm focused on accumulating and compounding Solana (SOL) in their treasury strategy, revealed plans to conduct a live X Spaces gathering on January 29, 2025, at 11:30 AM Eastern Time.

data.title: Jeff Glickman joins Neal Gerber Eisenberg as Partner in Private Wealth, bringing expertise in estate planning and tax strategies, enhancing firm's practice.
data.description: In Chicago, Neal Gerber Eisenberg (NGE) welcomes Jeffrey (Jeff) D. Glickman as a new partner in its Private Wealth practice group. Bringing with him expertise in estate planning, wealth transfer techniques, and trust administration, Jeff enriches NGE's offerings in this area. With a focus on intricate financial planning and substantial tax strategy experience, Jeff enhances the practice's capabilities. His role involves developing comprehensive strategies for clients in the realm of estate planning and wealth management.

data.title: Baddeck LTC workers at Alderwood Rest Home vote 99% in favor of strike for better wages and job retention, joining over 30 CUPE locals.
data.description: In Baddeck, Nova Scotia, employees at Alderwood Rest Home, who are members of the Canadian Union of Public Employees (CUPE) 1635, have overwhelmingly voted in favor of a strike mandate with 99% support. This decision was made in response to over 30 other CUPE long-term care locals also demanding better wages and retention policies. CUPE 1635 President, Elsa Rob, highlighted the challenging conditions in long-term care by emphasizing the significant physical, emotional, and financial toll faced by workers. This collective action by thousands of workers reflects the urgent need for improvements in the long-term care sector.

data.title: Norris McLaughlin, P.A. adds real estate attorneys H. Michael Lynch, Kimberly Yeager, and Chavala Kobluk from Lynch and Associates.
data.description: On January 28, 2026, in New York, the law firm of Norris McLaughlin, P.A. welcomed a strong group of real estate attorneys to their team. This includes H. Michael Lynch, Esq. and Kimberly Yeager, Esq. who were previously with Lynch and Associates in Manhattan and Long Island. This addition further bolsters the firm's real estate expertise.

data.title: Fed may pause rate cuts due to strong economy. Powell's term ends on May 15 but may continue as Fed governor. S&P 500 fluctuates.
data.description: Chairman Powell's term is set to conclude on May 15, but there is a possibility for him to continue as a Federal Reserve governor. The Federal Reserve's decision to halt rate cuts may be prolonged due to the strong state of the economy. The S&P 500 index fluctuates amid this development.

data.title: University of Phoenix webinar on building high-performing teams in AI era offers insights for leaders. Date: Jan. 28, 2026.
data.description: In the upcoming webinar on Jan. 28, 2026, the University of Phoenix will provide practical insights for leaders dealing with workforce trends influenced by artificial intelligence, organizational transformations, and shifting employee demands. This event is part of the Bridging Perspectives series and aims to offer guidance on navigating these challenges. This webinar is crucial for leaders seeking to understand the impact of AI and evolving expectations on their organizations.

data.title: Dogecoin and Shiba Inu are consolidating, DOGE dropped 7% to $0.1175-$0.1287, rebounding to $0.1250. Shib is in a similar position with no clear breakout catalyst.
data.description: Dogecoin and Shiba Inu are currently trading in narrow ranges following significant declines, lacking a clear reason for a breakout. Dogecoin (DOGE) is consolidating between $0.1175 and $0.1287 after a 7% drop from $0.1260 to around $0.1175, where buyers intervened to push the price back to approximately $0.1250. DOGE is slightly above the 0.5 Fibonacci retracement at $0.1232 and aiming to reclaim the 0.618 level near $0.1244. The hourly Relative Strength Index (RSI) sits at 56.20, indicating neutral momentum with a slight bullish bias after recovering from oversold territory during the previous day's selloff.

data.title: Another potential U.S. government shutdown looms if funding isn't agreed upon by Jan. 30, 2026, prompting travel insurance advice from Squaremouth.
data.description: In St. Petersburg, Florida, as of January 28, 2026, the potential for another U.S. government shutdown looms if Congress fails to agree on a funding arrangement by January 30, 2026. Squaremouth, a prominent travel insurance comparison platform, aims to educate travelers on how this impending situation could impact them.
